body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 55/2026
Made under: Employment And Assistance Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: April 7, 2026

B.C. Reg. 55/2026, deposited April 7, 2026, under theEMPLOYMENT AND ASSISTANCE ACT[section 35 (2)]. Order in Council 133/2026, approved and ordered April 7, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective April 15, 2026, the Employment and Assistance Regulation, B.C. Reg. 263/2002, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — S. MALCOLMSON,Minister of Social Development and Poverty Reduction; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 65 of the Employment and Assistance Regulation, B.C. Reg. 263/2002, is amended (a)	in subsection (1) by repealing the definition of"funeral costs"and substituting the following: "funeral costs"means the costs, as set out in or determined in accordance with Schedule F, of the following items: (i)	provided by a person who, under theCremation, Interment and Funeral Services Act, is a licensed funeral provider or is exempt from being licensed to provide funeral services, and (ii)	listed in section 2 (1) of Schedule F, provided in the course of providing funeral services within the meaning of (A)	paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition of "funeral services" in section 1 of theCremation, Interment and Funeral Services Act, or (B)	paragraph (d) of that definition, to the extent that it relates to paragraph (a) or (b) of that definition; (b)	intraprovincial transportation costs; (c)	cremation or burial of a deceased person’s body or remains, including the cost of a casket or urn;, and (b)	by repealing subsection (3.1) (a) and (b) and substituting the following: (a)	in respect of a particular item or service that is a funeral cost, (i)	the cost for the item or service as set out in or determined in accordance with Schedule F, or (ii)	if subparagraph (i) does not apply, the cost that is, in the opinion of the minister, the lowest reasonable cost for that item or service, and. 2	The following section is added: Transition – deaths before April 15, 2026 65.1(1)	Despite any amendment to section 65 made on or after April 15, 2026, a supplement payable under that section in relation to a person who died before that date is payable in accordance with that section as it read immediately before that date. (2)	This section is repealed on April 15, 2027.

minutes_briefs.title: BC updates funeral cost rules for income assistance
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has amended the Employment and Assistance Regulation to change how funeral costs are defined and calculated for income assistance recipients. The new rules, effective April 15, 2026, require funeral costs to be provided by a licensed funeral provider (or someone exempt from licensing) and follow a new Schedule F for determining eligible costs. The amendment also clarifies that intraprovincial transportation costs and cremation or burial costs (including caskets or urns) are covered. For deaths before April 15, 2026, the old rules still apply.

key_dates.label: New funeral cost rules for income assistance take effect in BC
Transition rule for pre-April 15 deaths expires in BC
